title: No Water in Condenser System
shortDescription: The dryer's condenser system is not receiving water for cooling.
fullDescription: Error E10 on Electrolux dryers with a water-cooled condenser indicates the unit is not detecting adequate water flow for condenser cooling. This error is specific to condenser dryers connected to a water supply.
symptoms: Clothes still damp at end of cycle
No water draining from drain hose

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the water inlet hose for kinks or damage. Straighten and ensure clear flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the inlet hose and clean the mesh filter at the valve connection.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check for voltage at the valve during operation. Replace if voltage present but no water flows.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E10 water supply error fix

faq.q: Does my Electrolux dryer use water?
faq.a: Condenser dryers with water-cooled condensers connect to a water supply for cooling efficiency.

faq.q: Can I use the dryer without the water connection?
faq.a: Some models can operate with an air-cooled condenser, but performance is reduced and the error must be reset.

faq.q: How do I know if the valve is bad?
faq.a: If the valve receives power during operation but no water flows, it needs replacement.

title: Drain Pump Failure
shortDescription: The condensate drain pump is not evacuating water from the collection tank.
fullDescription: Error E20 on Electrolux condenser dryers indicates the drain pump is not removing condensed water from the collection reservoir. This can cause the dryer to stop to prevent overflow.

causes.cause: Condensate collection tank full and not emptied
Drain pump blocked by lint or debris

symptoms: Pump makes no sound or strained noise

fixSteps.title: Empty condensate tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the condensate collection tank. Rinse clean and reinsert.

fixSteps.title: Clean pump and housing
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump area and clear any lint or debris from the pump and its housing.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: If using continuous drain, inspect the hose for kinks or blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test pump continuity. Replace if motor has failed.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E20 drain pump condensate error fix

faq.q: How often should I empty the water tank?
faq.a: After every drying cycle, or set up continuous drainage with a hose to your plumbing.

faq.q: Can lint cause E20?
faq.a: Yes, lint can accumulate in the pump and block it over time.

faq.q: Where is the drain pump located?
faq.a: Typically behind the lower front panel or underneath the drum on condenser models.

title: Door Lock Fault
shortDescription: The dryer cannot confirm the door is securely closed and locked.
fullDescription: Error E40 on Electrolux dryers indicates the door lock mechanism has not engaged properly or the door switch is not sending the correct closed signal to the control board. The dryer will not start until the door is confirmed locked.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or latched
Door latch mechanism worn or broken
Wiring to door lock assembly damaged

symptoms: Door light stays on with door closed

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and firmly close the door, ensuring it clicks into position.

fixSteps.title: Inspect latch and striker
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door latch hook and the striker plate for wear, damage, or misalignment.

fixSteps.title: Test door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the door switch for continuity when the door is closed.

fixSteps.title: Replace door latch or switch
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the worn or faulty component. Ensure the new latch aligns with the striker.

whenToCallPro: If the door frame or hinge is damaged causing misalignment.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E40 door lock error fix

faq.q: Why won't my Electrolux dryer start?
faq.a: E40 means the door is not registering as closed. Check the latch and switch.

faq.q: Can a worn latch cause E40?
faq.a: Yes, a worn latch may not engage the switch fully enough to signal the board.

faq.q: Is the door switch easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes, it is usually held by one or two screws and a plug connector behind the front panel.

title: Drive Motor Fault
shortDescription: The control board has detected a problem with the drum drive motor.
fullDescription: Error E50 on Electrolux dryers indicates the drum motor is drawing excessive current, not running, or the motor sensing circuit has detected a fault. The drum will not rotate.

causes.cause: Drive belt broken or slipped off
Drum seized due to foreign object or bearing failure

symptoms: Dryer shuts off immediately after starting

fixSteps.title: Check drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer and inspect the belt. If broken or loose, replace it.

fixSteps.title: Spin drum by hand
fixSteps.instruction: With the belt off, try spinning the drum by hand. It should rotate freely. Investigate if stiff or stuck.

fixSteps.title: Test motor and capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor winding resistance and capacitor value with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor or capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component. Motor replacement requires removing the belt and fan assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the motor windings are damaged or the motor needs full replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E50 motor drum error fix

faq.q: Can a broken belt cause E50?
faq.a: Yes, if the motor runs but the drum does not turn, a broken belt is the most common cause.

faq.q: What does a humming motor mean?
faq.a: A humming motor that does not turn usually indicates a failed capacitor or seized bearing.

faq.q: How long does a dryer motor last?
faq.a: Typically 10 to 15 years depending on usage and maintenance.

title: Heating System Fault
shortDescription: The dryer has detected a problem with the heating element or heating circuit.
fullDescription: Error E60 on Electrolux dryers indicates the heating system is not functioning correctly. The element may be open circuit, the thermal fuse may have blown, or the heating relay on the control board has failed.

causes.cause: Heating relay on control board failed

symptoms: Clothes take very long to dry

fixSteps.title: Check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thermal fuse on the heater housing and test with a multimeter. Replace if open circuit.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the element. Should read 15 to 40 ohms depending on model.

fixSteps.title: Check NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the NTC sensor resistance at room temperature. Should be approximately 10k ohms at 25C.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the element, fuse, or sensor as diagnosed.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected to be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E60 heating element no heat error fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer blow cold air?
faq.a: E60 means the heating system has failed. Test the element and thermal fuse first.

faq.q: Can a clogged vent cause E60?
faq.a: A blocked vent can cause overheating which blows the thermal fuse, leading to E60.

faq.q: Should I replace the thermal fuse and element together?
faq.a: Replace the fuse. Only replace the element if testing confirms it has failed.

title: NTC Sensor Out of Range
shortDescription: The temperature sensor is reading outside the expected range.
fullDescription: Error E70 on Electrolux dryers indicates the NTC thermistor sensor is providing temperature readings that are outside the valid operating range. The control board cannot regulate heating safely.

causes.cause: NTC sensor failed open or short circuit
Control board sensor input circuit fault

symptoms: Dryer overheats or does not heat at all

fixSteps.title: Clean sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove lint accumulation around the NTC sensor to ensure accurate readings.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should be around 10k ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the sensor to the control board for breaks or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor reads open or short, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine, suggesting a control board issue.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E70 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: A Negative Temperature Coefficient thermistor that measures drum air temperature by changing resistance with temperature.

faq.q: Can E70 cause overheating?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads incorrectly the control board may not shut off the heater at the right time.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, typically 8 to 20 dollars for the part.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board and user interface board have lost communication.
fullDescription: Error E90 on Electrolux dryers indicates a communication failure between the main electronic control board and the front panel user interface. Buttons may become unresponsive and the display may show garbled information.
symptoms: Dryer does not start from panel

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect from power for 5 minutes. Reconnect and test.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top panel and reseat the flat cable connecting the UI board to the main board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect boards for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check both boards for burn marks, swollen capacitors, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the UI board or main control board as needed.

whenToCallPro: If reseating the cable does not fix the error.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E90 communication board error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E90?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage the communication circuits on either board.

faq.q: Is reseating the cable a common fix?
faq.a: Yes, vibration from the dryer can loosen the ribbon cable connection over time.

faq.q: Which board is more likely to fail?
faq.a: The user interface board fails more often due to heat exposure from the dryer.

title: Power Supply Frequency Error
shortDescription: The control board has detected an abnormal power supply frequency.
fullDescription: Error EH0 on Electrolux dryers indicates the incoming mains power frequency is outside the acceptable range. The control board expects 50Hz or 60Hz depending on region and has detected significant deviation.

causes.cause: Generator power with poor frequency regulation
EMI filter on control board failed
Main control board power sensing circuit fault

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the wall outlet voltage and frequency with a multimeter. Ensure it matches local standards.

fixSteps.title: Eliminate interference
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the dryer directly into a wall outlet, not a power strip or extension cord.

fixSteps.title: Test with different outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Try a different circuit to rule out wiring issues in the original outlet.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board
fixSteps.instruction: If power supply tests normal, the board EMI filter or sensing circuit may be faulty. Requires board replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the power supply tests normal and the board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer EH0 power frequency error fix

faq.q: What power frequency does my dryer need?
faq.a: In North America 60Hz, in Europe and most other regions 50Hz.

faq.q: Can a generator cause EH0?
faq.a: Yes, portable generators often have poor frequency stability which triggers this error.

faq.q: Is EH0 dangerous?
faq.a: The dryer is protecting itself from abnormal power. Check your electrical supply if this error persists.

title: UI Board Communication Timeout
shortDescription: The user interface board has stopped responding to the main control board.
fullDescription: Error E91 is a specific communication timeout where the main board sent a request to the UI board but received no response within the allowed time. This differs from E90 which is a general communication loss.

causes.cause: UI board processor hang or crash

symptoms: Error may clear on its own

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, then reconnect. A processor hang is cleared by power cycling.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the panel and firmly reseat all cable connections between the boards.

fixSteps.title: Check for EMI sources
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dryer is not near strong electromagnetic sources like large motors or welders.

fixSteps.title: Replace UI board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after reseating, replace the user interface board.

whenToCallPro: If the UI board needs replacement and you are not comfortable opening the panel.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E91 UI board timeout error fix

faq.q: Is E91 the same as E90?
faq.a: They are related. E91 is a specific timeout while E90 is a general communication loss.

faq.q: Can E91 fix itself?
faq.a: Sometimes. A processor hang may clear on a power cycle and not return.

faq.q: How do I know if it is the cable or the board?
faq.a: If reseating the cable fixes it, the cable was loose. If it returns, the board likely needs replacing.

title: Inverter Board Fault
shortDescription: The inverter control board for the motor has detected a fault condition.
fullDescription: Error E5A on Electrolux dryers with inverter-driven motors indicates a failure in the inverter board that controls motor speed and direction. The drum motor cannot be operated safely.

causes.cause: Motor winding fault causing inverter overload

fixSteps.title: Cool down period
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dryer and let it cool for 30 minutes. Overheating can cause temporary faults.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the area around the dryer has adequate airflow. Clean any dust from vents and the back panel.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inverter board
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear panel and visually inspect the inverter board for burn marks or damaged components.

fixSteps.title: Replace inverter board
fixSteps.instruction: If damaged, replace the entire inverter board assembly.

whenToCallPro: Recommended as inverter board diagnostics require specialized knowledge.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dryer E5A inverter board motor error fix

faq.q: What is an inverter dryer?
faq.a: Inverter dryers use variable-speed motor control for quieter and more energy-efficient operation.

faq.q: Can overheating cause E5A?
faq.a: Yes, if the dryer is in a confined space without adequate ventilation, the inverter board can overheat.

faq.q: Is the inverter board expensive?
faq.a: Typically 60 to 140 dollars for the part, plus labor if professionally repaired.
